diagrams.title: Classical Planning Assumptions
diagrams.description: Shows the three key assumptions that define classical planning: Fully Observable, Deterministic, Static
elements: Fully Observable box - complete knowledge of world state
Static box - world changes only through agent actions
All three connect to Planning Problem in center
diagrams.purpose: Visualize and remember the foundational assumptions of classical planning

diagrams.title: Planning Problem Components
diagrams.description: Formal definition of a planning problem showing Initial State, Actions (with Preconditions and Effects), Goal State
elements: Actions box with Preconditions and Effects
Arrow showing Plan connecting Initial to Goal
diagrams.purpose: Understand the formal structure of a planning problem

diagrams.title: Planning Agent Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows how a planning agent operates: observe state, formulate plan, execute, repeat until goal
diagrams.purpose: Understand how a planning agent works cyclically

visuals.title: Definition Of Classical Planning
visuals.description: Visual diagram for definition of classical planning
